title: Grammys 2026: Bad Bunny Makes History With Album of the Year, Kendrick Lamar Becomes Most-Awarded Rapper Ever
text: Hollywood Reporter coverage of the 2026 Grammy Awards highlights and major winners. Major Category Winners: Album of the Year went to Bad Bunny for DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS, making him the first-ever artist to win the Grammy for album of the year for a non-English language album. Best Rap Album was won by Kendrick Lamar for GNX, defeating entries from Clipse and Tyler, the Creator. Record of the Year was claimed by Kendrick Lamar for his collaboration with SZA, Luther. Song of the Year went to Billie Eilish for Wildflower from her 2024 album Hit Me Hard and Soft. Best New Artist was won by Olivia Dean. Historic Achievement: Kendrick Lamar earned five Grammy wins total, becoming the most-awarded rapper in Grammy history, surpassing Jay-Z. He won best rap album and best rap song among other categories. Political Undertone: The ceremony featured significant activism, with multiple artists calling out ICE and immigration enforcement issues affecting Minneapolis.
